creator: neldredge@math.ucsd.edu
text:
Hi folks,

I think I have encountered this bug.

My amd64 box has an SATA hard disk with some bad sectors.  When you try to 
read these sectors (e.g. with "dd if=/dev/ad4 of=/dev/null bs=1024k"), the 
following messages are printed:

ad4: WARNING - SETFEATURES SET TRANSFER MODE taskqueue timeout - completing request directly
ad4: TIMEOUT - READ_DMA retrying (1 retry left) LBA=9464064

The kernel then panics with a page fault.  I couldn't gather the complete 
traceback but it looks like this:

device_attach+0x22
bus_generic_attach+0x18
ata_identify+0xea
ata_sata_phy_event+0xe2
taskqueue_run+0xd7
taskqueue_thread_loop+0x85
fork_exit+0x86 (or maybe 0x80, I can't quite read my handwriting)
fork_trampoline+0xe

The faulting instruction is "cmp $0, 0x50(%r13)", and %r13=0.

A glance at the source reveals that the problem is that in device_attach, 
dev->devclass is NULL.  (device_attach calls device_sysctl_init which 
calls devclass_sysctl_init, where dev->devclass is actually dereferenced. 
They all appear in device_attach due to inlining.)  I presume 
device_attach is getting called because the request for the bad sector 
fails and then the kernel tries to reset the device.

This is completely reproducible, with slightly different LBA numbers.

Here's some system info.

kernel: FreeBSD vulcan.lan 6.2-RELEASE-p7 FreeBSD 6.2-RELEASE-p7 #7: Mon Sep 17 22:12:36 PDT 2007     root@vulcan.lan:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/VULCAN  amd64
disk: Maxtor DiamondMax 10 (model 6B250S0), 250 GB SATA
SATA controller: Silicon Image SiI3114
Motherboard: Tyan S2875 (Tiger K8W)

dmesg output follows.  I don't have boot_verbose on, sorry.  I cannot 
reboot right now to get it, but I can do it tomorrow if something 
important is missing.
